protocol_design_brief.title: Draft Protocol Design Brief for NCT04903795

executive_synopsis.title: Executive Synopsis
executive_synopsis.body: Draft strategy brief for hEGFRvIII-CD3 (BRiTE) in glioblastoma. The target registry record is NCT04903795, PHASE1, and this artifact requires human review before protocol use.

strategic_rationale.title: Strategic Rationale
strategic_rationale.body: Rationale is grounded in Agent 3 clinical-risk context, Agent 4 diligence findings, and public analog trial benchmarks. Endpoint risk is low; missing or low-confidence upstream items are carried as flags.

analog_trial_benchmark_summary.title: Analog Trial Benchmark Summary
analog_trial_benchmark_summary.body: Analog benchmark uses 10 selected CT.gov analog trials. Enrollment median 39.0 participants, mean 52.8, range 7.0-160.0, IQR 39.0, missing 0; planned duration median 44.8 months, mean 44.0, range 19.3-79.4, IQR 22.93, missing 0. Primary endpoint families: other: 19, safety: 8, survival: 4, response: 3. Comparator categories: active_comparator_or_combination: 7, single_arm_or_uncontrolled: 3. Limitations and missing fields are carried as flags.

target_population.title: Target Population
target_population.body: Draft target population follows the public target trial record: condition Newly Diagnosed Glioblastoma, Recurrent Glioblastoma; sex ALL; minimum age 18 Years; maximum age unknown. Human review should confirm biomarker, line-of-therapy, organ function, and safety exclusions.

study_design.title: Study Design
study_design.body: Draft design should be benchmarked against selected analog trials for randomization, blinding, arm count, duration, and enrollment burden; no final protocol design decision is made by Agent 5.

comparator_and_landscape_rationale.title: Comparator And Landscape Rationale
comparator_and_landscape_rationale.body: Comparator rationale is based on named comparators and control categories detected in selected CT.gov analog trials plus Agent 4 competitive-landscape context.

endpoint_strategy.title: Endpoint Strategy
endpoint_strategy.body: Draft endpoint strategy should align with analog endpoint-family frequencies and the target trial primary endpoint family: response. Statistical review is required before endpoint hierarchy or powering assumptions are used.

draft_eligibility_framework.title: Draft Eligibility Framework
draft_eligibility_framework.body: Draft eligibility framework should start from analog themes. Inclusion themes: age; diagnosis; consent; performance status; measurable disease. Exclusion themes: infection; pregnant; organ function; cardiac; prior therapy; metastases. Safety themes: infection; pregnant; organ function; cardiac; qt; toxicity.

draft_schedule_of_assessments_framework.title: Draft Schedule Of Assessments Framework
draft_schedule_of_assessments_framework.body: Draft schedule should cover screening, baseline, treatment visits, response/safety assessments, biomarker or diagnostic testing when applicable, end-of-treatment, and follow-up. Visit timing remains a human-reviewed design choice.

safety_monitoring_outline.title: Safety Monitoring Outline
safety_monitoring_outline.body: Safety monitoring should incorporate openFDA label context when available and safety exclusion themes from analog trial eligibility criteria. This is a review prompt, not a final safety plan.

statistical_analysis_skeleton.title: Statistical Analysis Skeleton
statistical_analysis_skeleton.body: Statistical skeleton should define estimand, analysis population, primary analysis method, multiplicity, interim review, and missing-data handling after biostatistician review; Agent 5 does not invent powering assumptions.

operational_feasibility_risks.title: Operational Feasibility Risks
operational_feasibility_risks.body: Operational risks should be reviewed against analog enrollment, duration, site/country distribution, biomarker testing, prior-treatment restrictions, and schedule burden.

regulatory_standards_considerations.title: Regulatory Standards Considerations
regulatory_standards_considerations.body: Regulatory considerations are limited to questions for human review, including endpoint acceptability, comparator justification, eligibility defensibility, safety monitoring, and statistical analysis alignment.
